metadata.document_title: Immediate Release Solid Oral Dosage Forms: Scale-Up and Postapproval Changes: Chemistry, Manufacturing, and Controls, In Vitro Dissolution Testing, and In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation (SUPAC-IR)
metadata.notes: SUPAC-IR (1995, CMC 5) covers immediate release solid oral dosage forms. Distinct from SUPAC-MR (1997, CMC 8) already in output-v5/19-supac-ir-post-approval-changes-rules.json. No prior extraction found in v2/v3/v4. Supersedes OGDPPC Guide 22-90 section 4.G for IR dosage forms. Three change levels: Level 1 (unlikely impact), Level 2 (significant impact), Level 3 (likely significant impact). Four change categories: Components/Composition (III), Site (IV), Batch Size (V), Manufacturing (VI).

rules.rule_text: Sponsors of NDAs, ANDAs, and AADAs who intend, during the postapproval period, to change (1) the components or composition; (2) the site of manufacture; (3) the scale-up/scale-down of manufacture; and/or (4) the manufacturing (process and equipment) of an immediate release oral formulation must comply with this SUPAC-IR guidance. This guidance supersedes the recommendations in section 4.G of OGDPPC Guide 22-90 for immediate release dosage forms.
rules.source_section: Section I — Purpose of Guidance
rules.verbatim_quote: This guidance provides recommendations to sponsors of new drug applications (NDA's), abbreviated new drug applications (ANDA's), and abbreviated antibiotic applications (AADA's) who intend, during the postapproval period, to change: 1) the components or composition; 2) the site of manufacture; 3) the scale-up/scale-down of manufacture; and/or 4) the manufacturing (process and equipment) of an immediate release oral formulation.
exceptions: Does not apply to changes in drug substance amounts

rules.rule_text: The SUPAC-IR guidance defines three levels of change with increasing regulatory burden: Level 1 changes are unlikely to have any detectable impact on formulation quality and performance; Level 2 changes could have a significant impact on formulation quality and performance; Level 3 changes are likely to have a significant impact on formulation quality and performance. For each level, the guidance specifies required chemistry/CMC documentation, dissolution testing, and in vivo bioequivalence documentation.
rules.source_section: Section I — Purpose of Guidance
rules.verbatim_quote: The guidance defines: 1) levels of change; 2) recommended chemistry, manufacturing, and controls tests for each level of change; 3) in vitro dissolution tests and/or in vivo bioequivalence tests for each level of change; and 4) documentation that should support the change.

rules.rule_text: For changes filed in a 'changes being effected supplement' under 21 CFR 314.70(c), FDA may, after a review of the supplemental information, decide that the changes are not approvable. Sponsors must contact the appropriate CDER review division for multiple changes submitted at one time or over a short period of time, or where the number of batches needed for stability testing is not specified in this guidance.
rules.source_section: Section I — Purpose of Guidance
rules.verbatim_quote: For those changes filed in a 'changes being effected supplement' [21 CFR 314.70(c)], the FDA may, after a review of the supplemental information, decide that the changes are not approvable.

rules.rule_text: Pilot scale for solid oral dosage forms is defined as the manufacture of either drug substance or drug product by a procedure fully representative of and simulating that used for full manufacturing scale. For solid oral dosage forms this is generally taken to be, at a minimum, one-tenth that of full production, or 100,000 tablets or capsules, whichever is larger.
rules.source_section: Section II.K — Definition of Pilot Scale
rules.verbatim_quote: For solid oral dosage forms this is generally taken to be, at a minimum, one-tenth that of full production, or 100,000 tablets or capsules, whichever is larger.

rules.rule_text: A significant body of information on the stability of the drug product is likely to exist after five years of commercial experience for new molecular entities, or three years of commercial experience for new dosage forms. This definition determines whether reduced stability batches (1 batch with 3-month accelerated data) or full stability packages (up to 3 batches) are required for Level 3 changes.
rules.source_section: Section II.R — Definition of Significant Body of Information
rules.verbatim_quote: A significant body of information on the stability of the drug product is likely to exist after five years of commercial experience for new molecular entities, or three years of commercial experience for new dosage forms.

rules.rule_text: Components and composition Level 1 changes — unlikely to have any detectable impact on formulation quality and performance — include: (a) deletion or partial deletion of an ingredient intended to affect only color or flavor, or change in printing ink ingredient to another approved ingredient; and (b) changes in excipients expressed as percentage (w/w) of total formulation within the following ranges: Filler ±5%, Disintegrant (Starch) ±3%, Disintegrant (Other) ±1%, Binder ±0.5%, Lubricant (Ca/Mg Stearate) ±0.25%, Lubricant (Other) ±1%, Glidant (Talc) ±1%, Glidant (Other) ±0.1%, Film Coat ±1%. Total additive effect of all excipient changes must not exceed 5%.
rules.source_section: Section III.A — Level 1 Changes: Components and Composition
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 1 changes are those that are unlikely to have any detectable impact on formulation quality and performance. The total additive effect of all excipient changes should not be more than 5%.
exceptions: Percentages based on assumption drug substance formulated to 100% of label/potency

rules.rule_text: For components and composition Level 1 changes, the required documentation is: Chemistry Documentation — application/compendial release requirements plus stability testing of one batch on long-term stability data reported in the annual report. Dissolution Documentation — none beyond application/compendial requirements. In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation — none. Filing: Annual report (all information including long-term stability data).
rules.source_section: Section III.A.2-3 — Level 1 Test and Filing Documentation
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability testing: one batch on long-term stability data reported in annual report. Dissolution Documentation: None beyond application/compendial requirements. In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation: None. Filing Documentation: Annual report (all information including long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: Components and composition Level 2 changes — could have significant impact on formulation quality and performance — include: (a) change in technical grade of an excipient (e.g., Avicel PH102 vs. PH200); and (b) changes in excipients exceeding Level 1 ranges but within Level 2 ranges (representing a twofold increase over Level 1): Filler ±10%, Disintegrant (Starch) ±6%, Disintegrant (Other) ±2%, Binder ±1%, Lubricant (Ca/Mg Stearate) ±0.5%, Lubricant (Other) ±2%, Glidant (Talc) ±2%, Glidant (Other) ±0.2%, Film Coat ±2%. Total additive effect must not change by more than 10%. Tests and filing documentation vary based on therapeutic range (narrow vs. non-narrow), solubility (high: dose/solubility volume ≤250 mL; low: >250 mL), and permeability (high: absorption >90%; low).
rules.source_section: Section III.B — Level 2 Changes: Components and Composition
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 2 changes are those that could have a significant impact on formulation quality and performance. Tests and filing documentation for a Level 2 change vary depending on three factors: therapeutic range, solubility, and permeability. The total additive effect of all excipient changes should not change by more than 10%.

rules.rule_text: For components and composition Level 2 changes, dissolution documentation requirements by drug category are: Case A (High Permeability, High Solubility) — dissolution of 85% in 15 minutes in 900 mL of 0.1N HCl; if fails, perform Case B or C. Case B (Low Permeability, High Solubility) — multi-point dissolution profile in application/compendial medium at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes or until asymptote reached; proposed and current formulation profiles should be similar. Case C (High Permeability, Low Solubility) — multi-point dissolution profiles in water, 0.1N HCl, and USP buffer media at pH 4.5, 6.5, and 7.5 (five profiles); sampling at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes until 90% dissolved or asymptote; profiles should be similar.
rules.source_section: Section III.B.2.b — Level 2 Dissolution Documentation
rules.verbatim_quote: Case A: High Permeability, High Solubility Drugs: Dissolution of 85% in 15 minutes in 900 mL of 0.1N HCl. Case B: Low Permeability, High Solubility Drugs: Multi-point dissolution profile should be performed in the application/compendial medium at 15, 30, 45, 60 and 120 minutes or until an asymptote is reached. Case C: High Permeability, Low Solubility Drugs: Multi-point dissolution profiles should be performed in water, 0.1 N HCl, and USP buffer media at pH 4.5, 6.5, and 7.5.

rules.rule_text: For components and composition Level 2 changes, filing documentation is: Prior Approval Supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); Annual Report (long-term stability data). Stability testing requires 1 batch with 3 months accelerated stability data in supplement and 1 batch on long-term stability. In vivo bioequivalence documentation — none required unless situation does not meet Case A, B, or C dissolution criteria, in which case refer to Level 3 requirements.
rules.source_section: Section III.B.2-3 — Level 2 Test and Filing Documentation
rules.verbatim_quote: In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation: None: if the situation does not meet the description in Case A, Case B or Case C, refer to Level 3 changes. Filing Documentation: Prior approval supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); annual report (long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: Components and composition Level 3 changes — likely to have significant impact on formulation quality and performance — include: (a) any qualitative and quantitative excipient changes to a narrow therapeutic drug beyond the Level 1 ranges; (b) all other drugs not meeting dissolution criteria under Level 2 Case B; (c) changes in excipient ranges of low solubility, low permeability drugs beyond Level 1 ranges; and (d) changes in excipient ranges of all drugs beyond Level 2 ranges. Level 3 requires full bioequivalence study. The bioequivalence study may be waived if an acceptable in vivo/in vitro correlation has been verified.
rules.source_section: Section III.C — Level 3 Changes: Components and Composition
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 3 changes are those that are likely to have a significant impact on formulation quality and performance. In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation: Full bioequivalence study. The bioequivalence study may be waived with an acceptable in vivo/in vitro correlation has been verified.
exceptions: BE waiver available if verified IVIVC
cross_references: Appendix A — Narrow Therapeutic Range Drugs

rules.rule_text: For components and composition Level 3 changes, filing documentation is: Prior Approval Supplement (all information including accelerated stability data); Annual Report (long-term stability data). Stability testing requirements depend on significant body of information availability: if available — 1 batch with 3-month accelerated data in supplement plus 1 batch long-term in annual report; if not available — up to 3 batches with 3-month accelerated data in supplement plus 1 batch long-term in annual report. Dissolution: Case B dissolution profile.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.2-3 — Level 3 Test and Filing Documentation
rules.verbatim_quote: Significant body of information available: One batch with three months accelerated stability data reported in supplement; one batch on long-term stability data reported in annual report. Significant body of information not available: Up to three batches with three months accelerated stability data reported in supplement; one batch on long-term stability data reported in annual report.

rules.rule_text: All allowable changes in composition must be based on the approved target composition and not on previous Level 1 or Level 2 changes in composition. Components (active and excipients) in the formulation must have numerical targets representing the nominal composition, and all future changes are based on these approved target values.
rules.source_section: Sections III.A and III.B — Target Composition Baseline
rules.verbatim_quote: Allowable changes in the composition should be based on the approved target composition and not on previous Level 1 or Level 2 changes in the composition.

rules.rule_text: Site changes consist of changes in location of the site of manufacture for both company-owned and contract manufacturing facilities, and do not include scale-up changes, manufacturing changes, or changes in components or composition. New manufacturing locations must have a satisfactory current Good Manufacturing Practice (cGMP) inspection.
rules.source_section: Section IV — Site Changes
rules.verbatim_quote: Site changes consist of changes in location of the site of manufacture for both company-owned and contract manufacturing facilities and do not include any scale-up changes, changes in manufacturing (including process and/or equipment), or changes in components or composition. New manufacturing locations should have a satisfactory current Good Manufacturing Practice (CGMP) inspection.

rules.rule_text: Site changes Level 1 — changes within a single facility where the same equipment, SOPs, environmental conditions (temperature and humidity), controls, and personnel common to both manufacturing sites are used, and no changes are made to the manufacturing batch records except administrative information and location. Documentation: Chemistry — none beyond compendial requirements. Dissolution — none beyond compendial requirements. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Annual report.
rules.source_section: Section IV.A — Site Changes Level 1
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 1 changes consist of site changes within a single facility where the same equipment, standard operating procedures (SOP's), environmental conditions (e.g., temperature and humidity) and controls, and personnel common to both manufacturing sites are used, and where no changes are made to the manufacturing batch records, except for administrative information and the location of the facility.

rules.rule_text: Site changes Level 2 — changes within a contiguous campus, or between facilities in adjacent city blocks, where the same equipment, SOPs, environmental conditions, controls, and personnel common to both sites are used, and no changes made to manufacturing batch records except administrative information and location. Documentation: Chemistry — location of new site and updated batch records, 1 batch on long-term stability in annual report. Dissolution — none beyond compendial. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Changes being effected supplement; annual report (long-term stability).
rules.source_section: Section IV.B — Site Changes Level 2
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 2 changes consist of site changes within a contiguous campus, or between facilities in adjacent city blocks, where the same equipment, SOP's, environmental conditions (e.g., temperature and humidity) and controls, and personnel common to both manufacturing sites are used, and where no changes are made to the manufacturing batch records, except for administrative information and the location of the facility.

rules.rule_text: Site changes Level 3 — change in manufacturing site to a different campus (not on the same original contiguous site or where facilities not in adjacent city blocks). To qualify as Level 3: same equipment, SOPs, environmental conditions, and controls must be used at new site; no changes to manufacturing batch records except administrative information, location, and language translation. Stability: if significant body of data available — 1 batch with 3-month accelerated data in supplement + 1 batch long-term in annual report; if not available — up to 3 batches with 3-month accelerated data in supplement + up to 3 batches long-term in annual report. Dissolution: Case B. Filing: Changes being effected supplement; annual report (long-term).
rules.source_section: Section IV.C — Site Changes Level 3
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 3 changes consist of a change in manufacturing site to a different campus. A different campus is defined as one that is not on the same original contiguous site or where the facilities are not in adjacent city blocks. To qualify as a Level 3 change, the same equipment, SOP's, environmental conditions, and controls should be used in the manufacturing process at the new site, and no changes may be made to the manufacturing batch records except for administrative information, location and language translation, where needed.

rules.rule_text: Postapproval changes in batch size (scale-up or scale-down) from the pivotal/pilot scale biobatch require submission of additional information. Scale-down below 100,000 dosage units is not covered by this guidance. All scale-up changes must be properly validated and, where needed, inspected by appropriate agency personnel.
rules.source_section: Section V — Changes in Batch Size
rules.verbatim_quote: Postapproval changes in the size of a batch from the pivotal/pilot scale biobatch material to larger or smaller production batches call for submission of additional information in the application. Scale-down below 100,000 dosage units is not covered by this guidance. All scale-up changes should be properly validated and, where needed, inspected by appropriate agency personnel.
exceptions: Scale-down below 100,000 dosage units not covered

rules.rule_text: Batch size Level 1 changes — change in batch size up to and including a factor of 10 times the size of the pilot/biobatch, where: (1) equipment is of the same design and operating principles; (2) batches manufactured in full compliance with CGMPs; and (3) same SOPs and controls, same formulation and manufacturing procedures used on test batches and full-scale production batches. Documentation: Chemistry — application/compendial release requirements, notification of change and updated batch records in annual report, 1 batch on long-term stability in annual report. Dissolution — none beyond compendial. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Annual report.
rules.source_section: Section V.A — Batch Size Level 1
rules.verbatim_quote: Change in batch size, up to and including a factor of 10 times the size of the pilot/biobatch, where: 1) the equipment used to produce the test batch(es) is of the same design and operating principles; 2) the batch(es) is (are) manufactured in full compliance with CGMP's; and 3) the same standard operating procedures (SOP's) and controls, as well as the same formulation and manufacturing procedures, are used on the test batch(es) and on the full-scale production batch(es).

rules.rule_text: Batch size Level 2 changes — changes in batch size beyond a factor of 10 times the size of the pilot/biobatch, where: (1) same equipment design and operating principles; (2) full cGMP compliance; and (3) same SOPs, controls, formulation and manufacturing procedures on test and full-scale batches. Documentation: Chemistry — application/compendial release requirements, notification and updated batch records, 1 batch with 3-month accelerated stability plus 1 batch long-term stability. Dissolution: Case B. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Changes being effected supplement; annual report (long-term stability).
rules.source_section: Section V.B — Batch Size Level 2
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in batch size beyond a factor of ten times the size of the pilot/biobatch, where: 1) the equipment used to produce the test batch(es) is of the same design and operating principles; 2) the batch(es) is (are) manufactured in full compliance with CGMP'S; and 3) the same SOP's and controls as well as the same formulation and manufacturing procedures are used on the test batch(es) and on the full-scale production batch(es).

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing equipment Level 1 changes consist of: (1) change from non-automated or non-mechanical equipment to automated or mechanical equipment to move ingredients; and (2) change to alternative equipment of the same design and operating principles of the same or different capacity. Documentation: Chemistry — application/compendial release requirements, notification of change and updated batch records, 1 batch on long-term stability. Dissolution — none beyond compendial. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Annual report (long-term stability data).
rules.source_section: Section VI.A.1 — Equipment Level 1
rules.verbatim_quote: This category consists of: 1) change from non-automated or non-mechanical equipment to automated or mechanical equipment to move ingredients; and 2) change to alternative equipment of the same design and operating principles of the same or of a different capacity.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing equipment Level 2 changes consist of change in equipment to a different design and different operating principles. Documentation: Chemistry — application/compendial release requirements, notification and updated batch records, stability testing with significant body of data available (1 batch with 3-month accelerated in supplement; 1 batch long-term in annual report) or not available (up to 3 batches with 3-month accelerated in supplement; up to 3 batches long-term in annual report). Dissolution: Case C dissolution profile. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Prior approval supplement with justification; annual report (long-term stability).
rules.source_section: Section VI.A.2 — Equipment Level 2
rules.verbatim_quote: Change in equipment to a different design and different operating principles. Dissolution Documentation: Case C dissolution profile. Filing Documentation: Prior approval supplement with justification for change; annual report (long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing process Level 1 changes include process changes such as mixing times and operating speeds within application/validation ranges. Documentation: Chemistry — none beyond application/compendial release requirements. Dissolution — none beyond compendial. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Annual report.
rules.source_section: Section VI.B.1 — Process Level 1
rules.verbatim_quote: This category includes process changes including changes such as mixing times and operating speeds within application/validation ranges.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing process Level 2 changes include process changes such as mixing times and operating speeds outside of application/validation ranges. Documentation: Chemistry — application/compendial release requirements, notification and updated batch records, 1 batch on long-term stability. Dissolution: Case B dissolution profile. In vivo BE — none. Filing: Changes being effected supplement; annual report (long-term stability).
rules.source_section: Section VI.B.2 — Process Level 2
rules.verbatim_quote: This category includes process changes including changes such as mixing times and operating speeds outside of application/validation ranges.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturing process Level 3 changes include change in the type of process used in manufacture of the product, such as a change from wet granulation to direct compression of dry powder. Documentation: Chemistry — application/compendial release requirements, notification and updated batch records, stability testing (if significant body of data available: 1 batch with 3-month accelerated in supplement + 1 batch long-term in annual report; if not: up to 3 batches with 3-month accelerated + up to 3 batches long-term). Dissolution: Case B. In vivo BE — full bioequivalence study; may be waived if suitable IVIVC verified. Filing: Prior approval supplement with justification; annual report (long-term stability).
rules.source_section: Section VI.B.3 — Process Level 3
rules.verbatim_quote: This category includes change in the type of process used in the manufacture of the product, such as a change from wet granulation to direct compression of dry powder. In Vivo Bioequivalence Documentation: In vivo bioequivalence study. The bioequivalence study may be waived if a suitable in vivo/in vitro correlation has been verified.
exceptions: BE waiver available if verified IVIVC

rules.rule_text: All in vitro dissolution profiles must be conducted on at least 12 individual dosage units. Dissolution profiles may be compared using the similarity factor f2: f2 = 50 LOG {[1+1/n Σ(Rt-Tt)2]^-0.5 × 100}, where Rt and Tt are the percent dissolved at each time point. An f2 value between 50 and 100 suggests the two dissolution profiles are similar.
rules.source_section: Section VII — In Vitro Dissolution
rules.verbatim_quote: All profiles should be conducted on at least 12 individual dosage units. Dissolution profiles may be compared using the following equation that defines a similarity factor (f2): f2 = 50 LOG {[1+1/n Σ(Rt-Tt)2]-0.5 x 100} where Rt and Tt are the percent dissolved at each time point. An f2 value between 50 and 100 suggests the two dissolution profiles are similar.

rules.rule_text: Case A dissolution testing (for high permeability, high solubility drugs in Level 2 component/composition changes): dissolution of Q = 85% in 15 minutes in 900 mL of 0.1N hydrochloric acid (HCl), using USP <711> Apparatus 1 at 100 rpm or Apparatus 2 at 50 rpm.
rules.source_section: Section II.C — Dissolution Testing Case A Definition
rules.verbatim_quote: Case A: Dissolution of Q = 85% in 15 minutes in 900 milliliters (mL) of 0.1N hydrochloride (HCl), using the United States Pharmacopeia (USP) <711> Apparatus 1 at 100 revolutions per minute (rpm) or Apparatus 2 at 50 rpm.

rules.rule_text: Case B dissolution testing: multi-point dissolution profile in the application/compendial medium at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes or until an asymptote is reached for the proposed and currently accepted formulation.
rules.source_section: Section II.C — Dissolution Testing Case B Definition
rules.verbatim_quote: Case B: Multi-point dissolution profile in the application/compendial medium at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes or until an asymptote is reached for the proposed and currently accepted formulation.

rules.rule_text: Case C dissolution testing: multi-point dissolution profiles performed in water, 0.1N HCl, and USP buffer media at pH 4.5, 6.5, and 7.5 (five separate profiles) for the proposed and currently accepted formulations. Adequate sampling should be performed at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes until either 90% of drug from the drug product is dissolved or an asymptote is reached. A surfactant may be used with appropriate justification.
rules.source_section: Section II.C — Dissolution Testing Case C Definition
rules.verbatim_quote: Case C: Multi-point dissolution profiles performed in water, 0.1N HCl, and USP buffer media at pH 4.5, 6.5, and 7.5 (five separate profiles) for the proposed and currently accepted formulations. Adequate sampling should be performed at 15, 30, 45, 60, and 120 minutes until either 90% of drug from the drug product is dissolved or an asymptote is reached. A surfactant may be used with appropriate justification.

rules.rule_text: Drug solubility classification for SUPAC-IR: High solubility drugs are those with a dose/solubility volume of less than or equal to 250 mL. Solubility is calculated based on the minimum concentration (mg/mL) at the largest dosage strength, determined in the physiological pH range (pH 1 to 8) at 37 ± 0.5°C.
rules.source_section: Section III.B.1 — Level 2 Solubility Definition
rules.verbatim_quote: High solubility drugs are those with a dose/solubility volume of less than or equal to 250 mL. (Example: Compound A has as its lowest solubility at 37 ± 0.5°C, 1.0 mg/mL at pH 7, and is available in 100 mg, 200 mg and 400 mg strengths. This drug would be considered a low solubility drug as its dose/solubility volume is greater than 250 mL (400 mg/1.0 mg/mL=400 mL).)

rules.rule_text: Drug permeability classification for SUPAC-IR: High permeability drugs are generally those with an extent of absorption greater than 90% in the absence of documented instability in the gastrointestinal tract, or those whose permeability attributes have been determined experimentally. Permeability is the effective human jejunal wall permeability (Pe, cm/sec).
rules.source_section: Section III.B.1 — Level 2 Permeability Definition
rules.verbatim_quote: High permeability drugs are generally those with an extent of absorption greater than 90% in the absence of documented instability in the gastrointestinal tract, or those whose permeability attributes have been determined experimentally.

rules.rule_text: In vivo bioequivalence study design for SUPAC-IR: single dose, two-treatment, two-period crossover with adequate washout period between the two phases. Equal numbers of subjects should be randomly assigned to each of the two dosing sequences. Objective: to compare the rate and extent of absorption of the drug product for which manufacture has been changed to the drug product manufactured prior to the change.
rules.source_section: Section VIII — In Vivo Bioequivalence Studies
rules.verbatim_quote: The study design should be a single dose, two-treatment, two-period crossover with adequate washout period between the two phases of the study. Equal numbers of subjects should be randomly assigned to each of the two dosing sequences.

rules.rule_text: High solubility classification requires that the dose/solubility volume ≤250 mL. This is an early application of what became the Biopharmaceutics Classification System (BCS) framework. Narrow therapeutic range drugs listed in Appendix A require the most stringent change controls — any qualitative or quantitative excipient change beyond Level 1 ranges constitutes a Level 3 change requiring a full bioequivalence study.
rules.source_section: Section III.C.1.a and Appendix A — Narrow Therapeutic Range
rules.verbatim_quote: Any qualitative and quantitative excipient changes to a narrow therapeutic drug beyond the ranges noted in Section III.A.1.b.

rules.rule_text: The distinction between Level 1 and Level 2 excipient changes in batch composition is determined by whether the change exceeds the Level 1 percentage ranges (Filler ±5%, Disintegrant Starch ±3%, Other ±1%, Binder ±0.5%, Ca/Mg Stearate ±0.25%, Other lubricant ±1%, Glidant Talc ±1%, Other glidant ±0.1%, Film Coat ±1%) but stays within the Level 2 ranges (approximately twofold of Level 1: Filler ±10%, Disintegrant Starch ±6%, Other ±2%, Binder ±1%, Ca/Mg Stearate ±0.5%, Other lubricant ±2%, Glidant Talc ±2%, Other glidant ±0.2%, Film Coat ±2%). All percentages are w/w of total target dosage form weight at 100% label/potency.
rules.source_section: Sections III.A.1.b and III.B.1.b — Excipient Range Tables
rules.verbatim_quote: These percentages are based on the assumption that the drug substance in the product is formulated to 100% of label/potency. The total additive effect of all excipient changes should not be more than 5%.

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 site changes (contiguous campus or adjacent city blocks), the filing documentation is a changes being effected supplement, with annual report for long-term stability data. For Level 3 site changes (different campus), the filing documentation is also a changes being effected supplement, but requires more extensive stability testing. This differs from Level 3 composition and manufacturing changes which require prior approval supplements.
rules.source_section: Sections IV.B.3 and IV.C.3 — Site Change Filing
rules.verbatim_quote: Filing Documentation: Changes being effected supplement; annual report (long-term stability test data).

rules.rule_text: For Level 2 batch size changes (>10x pilot/biobatch), Case B dissolution testing is required. For Level 1 batch size changes (≤10x pilot/biobatch), no dissolution testing beyond application/compendial requirements is needed. All scale-up changes must be properly validated.
rules.source_section: Sections V.A and V.B — Batch Size Dissolution Requirements
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes in batch size beyond a factor of ten times the size of the pilot/biobatch. Dissolution Documentation: Case B testing.

rules.rule_text: Equipment changes to a different design and different operating principles (Level 2 equipment change) require a prior approval supplement with justification for the change, along with Case C dissolution profile and stability data. This is the most stringent documentation pathway for equipment changes.
rules.source_section: Section VI.A.2.c — Equipment Level 2 Filing
rules.verbatim_quote: Filing Documentation: Prior approval supplement with justification for change; annual report (long-term stability data).

rules.rule_text: Process changes within application/validation ranges (Level 1) require only an annual report. Process changes outside validation ranges but within the same process type (Level 2) require a changes being effected supplement. Changes in the type of manufacturing process (Level 3, e.g., wet granulation to direct compression) require a prior approval supplement, full bioequivalence study (unless IVIVC waiver applies), and Case B dissolution. This graduated filing requirement reflects the increasing risk to product quality.
rules.source_section: Sections VI.B.1-3 — Process Change Filing Summary
rules.verbatim_quote: Level 3: Filing Documentation: Prior approval supplement with justification; annual report (long-term stability data).
exceptions: Level 3 process BE waiver if IVIVC verified
